message:
test: add ~37 edge-case tests across block and mvcc crates

ffs-block (181 total): AlignedVec alignment, BlockBuf content equality,
CacheMetrics ratios, MemoryPressure target capacity, FlushDaemonConfig
validation, FlushPinToken, FaultMode/FaultTarget, ThrottleConfig defaults.

ffs-mvcc (257 total): CommitError clone/eq/display, BlockVersion serde,
PhysicalBlockVersion copy, EbrVersionStats/BlockVersionStats defaults
and serde, Transaction multi-block reads, MvccStore read-at-old-snapshot,
FCW conflict detection.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-2qf","title":"Repair: Extract FrankenSQLite RaptorQ approach and adapt to filesystem blocks","description":"Goal: reuse proven strategy from /dp/frankensqlite for fountain-code-based self-healing, but adapted to ext4/btrfs block groups and filesystem workloads.

Deliverables:
- Summarize the key FrankenSQLite design choices: symbol placement, overhead tuning, decode proofs, ledgering.
- Identify what changes for filesystems: block group boundaries, metadata vs data, read/write patterns.
- Update COMPREHENSIVE_SPEC repair section with an explicit design derived from this extraction.

Acceptance:
- We can explain exactly how a corrupted block is detected and which symbols are used to repair it.
- The design includes tunable parameters and an expected-loss justification (not ad-hoc thresholds).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:24:15.796807569Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T01:40:19.555409837Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T01:40:19.555343092Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["repair"]}
 {"id":"bd-2r8","title":"Implement adaptive backpressure system with budget propagation","description":"# Implement adaptive backpressure system with budget propagation

## GOAL
Implement Cx-based budget system that propagates backpressure through the stack, preventing cascade failures under load.

## BACKGROUND (Alien-Artifact Quality)
Traditional systems fail abruptly under load. FrankenFS must degrade gracefully using information-theoretic principles:

1. **Budget = scarce resource allocation**
   - deadline: wall-clock timeout
   - poll_quota: async poll iterations allowed
   - cost_quota: abstract "work units" allowed
   - priority: relative importance

2. **Budget combination**: min for limits, max for priority

3. **Backpressure = signal to reduce demand**
   When budget exhausted, callers must either:
   - Retry with fresh budget (if transient)
   - Propagate failure (if permanent)
   - Shed load (if graceful degradation enabled)

## TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS

### 1. Budget Types
```rust
pub struct Budget {
    pub deadline: Option<Instant>,
    pub poll_quota: Option<u64>,
    pub cost_quota: Option<u64>,
    pub priority: Priority,
}

impl Budget {
    pub fn combine(&self, other: &Budget) -> Budget {
        Budget {
            deadline: min_option(self.deadline, other.deadline),
            poll_quota: min_option(self.poll_quota, other.poll_quota),
            cost_quota: min_option(self.cost_quota, other.cost_quota),
            priority: max(self.priority, other.priority),
        }
    }

    pub fn is_exhausted(&self) -> bool {
        self.deadline.map_or(false, |d| Instant::now() > d)
            || self.poll_quota == Some(0)
            || self.cost_quota == Some(0)
    }
}
```

### 2. Integration Points
- **FUSE dispatch**: Per-request budget from config
- **ARC cache**: Eviction budget (prevent eviction storms)
- **MVCC commit**: Transaction budget
- **Scrub/GC**: Background budget (yields to foreground)
- **RaptorQ decode**: Symbol budget

### 3. Pressure Detection
```rust
pub struct PressureMonitor {
    pub fn sample_cpu_load() -> f64;
    pub fn sample_memory_pressure() -> f64;
    pub fn sample_io_depth() -> usize;
    pub fn compute_degradation_level() -> DegradationLevel;
}
```

### 4. Graceful Degradation FSM
```
Normal -> Pause Background -> Reduce Cache -> Throttle Writes -> Read-Only
   ^                                                              |
   +--------------------(pressure relieved)----------------------+
```

## TESTS
1. Unit: Budget combination semantics
2. Unit: Pressure detection thresholds
3. Integration: Simulated CPU load triggers degradation
4. Integration: Recovery when pressure relieved

## LOGGING
- Budget exhaustion (warn)
- Degradation level change (info)
- Pressure samples (trace)

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. Operations respect budget limits
2. Degradation level reflects actual pressure
3. Recovery is automatic and stable
4. No infinite loops or deadlocks under pressure","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:51:09.209435721Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T20:27:30.741671654Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T20:27:30.741640536Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","perf","reliability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2r8","depends_on_id":"bd-22w.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:53:02.370992350Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2r8","depends_on_id":"bd-3tz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:56:39.081031452Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2r8","depends_on_id":"bd-3tz.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:53:02.977623160Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

Initial slice: wire actionable write-side behavior and verification pass semantics.","notes":"Added bootstrap btrfs recovery for fsck/repair detection path: when initial detect fails and writes are allowed, CLI now restores primary superblock from validated backup mirror, re-detects, and continues; fsck repair status now reports bootstrap-performed; added two bootstrap regression tests; updated parity/readme wording.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-20T19:15:57.523409335Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T06:11:30.754252131Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T06:09:58.460084040Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["btrfs","cli","parity","repair"],"comments":[{"id":271,"issue_id":"bd-2rmw","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented first slice: btrfs --block-group scoped scrub support in fsck/repair using extent-tree discovery and logical->physical mapping; added mapping unit tests; full btrfs write-side recovery remains open.","created_at":"2026-02-20T19:30:44Z"},{"id":272,"issue_id":"bd-2rmw","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented next slice in crates/ffs-cli/src/main.rs: btrfs backup superblock mirror repair from primary for both repair and fsck --repair paths; added post-write re-scrub verification and two regression tests for mirror restoration. Validation currently blocked by pre-existing workspace failures unrelated to this diff (fmt drift + compile/clippy errors in ffs-extent/ffs-inode/ffs-mvcc/ffs-block).","created_at":"2026-02-22T03:17:49Z"},{"id":273,"issue_id":"bd-2rmw","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Added another bd-2rmw slice in ffs-cli: mirror superblock repair no longer depends on scrub findings (which only cover primary); it now validates in-scope btrfs mirror superblocks directly (read/parse/bytenr/checksum), repairs corrupted mirrors from a validated primary, and re-verifies. Added scoped tests: mirror repair skips when mirror is outside scope and repairs when inside scope.","created_at":"2026-02-22T03:58:16Z"},{"id":274,"issue_id":"bd-2rmw","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"SteelOtter session: Fixed all workspace compilation and clippy errors (ffs-mvcc persist.rs missing methods, ffs-core PersistCtx args, ffs-fuse borrow checker, ~15 clippy lints across 8 crates, ~51 test call sites). Wired RaptorQ symbol reconstruction into btrfs repair path: added group_btrfs_corrupt_blocks and recover_btrfs_corrupt_blocks in ffs-cli, integrated with GroupRecoveryOrchestrator. Full CI gate results: cargo fmt/check/clippy all pass (--all-targets), 477/478 tests pass (1 pre-existing flaky perf_regression_block_cache test fails on pristine HEAD too).","created_at":"2026-02-22T04:59:09Z"},{"id":275,"issue_id":"bd-2rmw","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Autonomous bugfix pass completed while advancing bd-2rmw workstream. Fixed failing suites uncovered during full-workspace runs: (1) crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs test resolve_extent_index_rejects_corrupted_child_depth corrected to corrupt actual child block, (2) crates/ffs-extent/src/lib.rs test fixture mock_pctx GDT block moved off block bitmap to avoid false double-free corruption, (3) crates/ffs-mvcc/src/lib.rs force_advance_oldest_snapshot now decrements a single refcount instead of dropping all refs, restoring intended backpressure semantics, (4) crates/ffs-ondisk/src/ext4.rs DX parser/test corrected for count/limit layout and entry offset. Gates now pass: fmt/check/clippy/workspace tests all green.","created_at":"2026-02-22T05:50:15Z"},{"id":276,"issue_id":"bd-2rmw","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"CalmBridge: Completed btrfs repair parity with ext4. (1) Implemented rebuild_btrfs_repair_symbols() mirroring ext4 workflow. (2) Wired RaptorQ block recovery into fsck --repair btrfs path (was superblock-only). (3) Fixed btrfs_repair_group_spec test (group too small). (4) Fixed pre-existing orphan inode test failures (extent data overlapped inode table). Full CI green except pre-existing perf_regression_block_cache (hw-specific baseline).","created_at":"2026-02-22T06:09:44Z"},{"id":277,"issue_id":"bd-2rmw","author":"SunnyElm","text":"Unblocked all-features gate by fixing ffs-block S3-FIFO queue bookkeeping and perf regression in crates/ffs-block/src/lib.rs. Root cause was pending-admission key being demoted/evicted during miss-side rebalance, plus one-pass main-queue second-chance overflow and tiny-capacity split underfill. Implemented pending-admission protection in T1/T2 rebalance, emergency clean-eviction fallback with dirty-overflow tolerance, tiny-capacity split relaxation, and bounded fast-hit promotion strategy to restore lookup_p99. Validation: cargo fmt --check; cargo check --workspace --all-targets; cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test -p ffs-block --all-features --lib; cargo test -p ffs-block --all-features --test perf_regression_e2e -- --nocapture; cargo test --workspace --all-features all passing.","created_at":"2026-02-22T06:11:30Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2s4","title":"EPIC: Production FUSE Runtime","description":"# EPIC: Production FUSE Runtime

## PURPOSE
Transform the FUSE interface from scaffold to production-ready mount runtime, as required by FEATURE_PARITY.md blocking gap #3.

## BACKGROUND
From spec §14 (FUSE Integration):
- Multi-threaded dispatch required
- All state must be Send + Sync
- Never block FUSE dispatch thread on raw I/O
- Proper lifecycle management (mount/unmount/signal handling)

Current state: "FUSE mount runtime: ❌ Interface scaffold only"

## CRITICAL REQUIREMENTS

### 1. Mount Lifecycle
- Clean mount with explicit options (ro/rw, cache_size, etc.)
- Graceful unmount with flush and cleanup
- Signal handling (SIGTERM, SIGINT -> clean unmount)
- Crash recovery on restart

### 2. Multi-threaded Dispatch
- Use fuser multi-threaded mode
- Thread pool sizing based on CPU count
- Per-thread ARC cache access (Send + Sync)
- Connection pooling for concurrent requests

### 3. Error Handling
- Every FUSE op maps to correct errno
- Timeout handling for hung operations
- Partial failure recovery

### 4. Performance
- Request batching where applicable
- Readahead for sequential access
- Writeback coalescing
- Attribute caching with configurable TTL

### 5. Observability
- Per-op latency metrics
- Request queue depth
- Error rate tracking

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. `ffs mount --rw /path/to/image.ext4 /mnt/ffs` works in production
2. Concurrent readers/writers do not deadlock
3. `umount /mnt/ffs` is clean (no data loss)
4. SIGTERM results in clean shutdown
5. All FUSE ops have correct errno mapping

## FILES
- crates/ffs-fuse/src/lib.rs (main implementation)
- crates/ffs-fuse/src/mount.rs (lifecycle)
- crates/ffs-fuse/src/dispatch.rs (multi-thread)
- crates/ffs-cli/src/main.rs (mount command)

## Success Criteria
1. All children closed
2. E2E suite (bd-3en1) passes
3. ffs mount --rw works on ext4 and btrfs images
4. SIGTERM unmount is clean (no data loss)
5. All FUSE ops have correct errno mapping","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:50:01.826764621Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-18T09:22:39.072572592Z","closed_at":"2026-02-18T09:22:39.072549739Z","close_reason":"Completed: all child beads closed and acceptance criteria validated by latest E2E artifacts","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","fuse","reliability"],"comments":[{"id":91,"issue_id":"bd-2s4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"SECOND-PASS REVIEW (lint fix): Epic is missing ## Success Criteria section. - Repro pack: env.json, manifest.json, repro.lock (and LEGAL.md if IP risk appears).","created_at":"2026-02-13T23:09:17Z"},{"id":209,"issue_id":"bd-2s4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Coordination note: claiming child bead bd-cebm for fsync/flush durability path work now. Implemented initial fsync/flush/fsyncdir wiring and durability sync behavior for ext4 path with passing full workspace gates; next pass should deepen integration tests/perf proof against the bead acceptance criteria.","created_at":"2026-02-14T00:44:11Z"},{"id":213,"issue_id":"bd-2s4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Child bd-cebm progress: durability + proof artifacts expanded with fsync crash-boundary checks and fsync latency benchmarks; workspace gates passing. Waiting on final integration acceptance before close.","created_at":"2026-02-14T04:27:35Z"},{"id":230,"issue_id":"bd-2s4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Progress update (FoggyRiver, 2026-02-18):\n- Investigated ext4 RW EIO repro artifacts and traced root inode mismatch: FUSE/VFS root inode=1 vs ext4 on-disk root inode=2.\n- Implemented ext4 inode aliasing at FsOps boundary in crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs:\n  - Canonicalize inbound ext4 inode args (1->2) for getattr/lookup/readdir/read/write/create/mkdir/unlink/rmdir/rename/link/symlink/fallocate/setattr/xattr paths.\n  - Present outbound ext4 attrs/dir entries back to VFS alias (2->1).\n- Added regression test tests::write_create_and_read_roundtrip_via_vfs_root_alias covering root getattr/readdir/create/write/read/lookup roundtrip via inode 1.\n- Validation (all via rch):\n  - cargo check -p ffs-core --all-targets : PASS\n  - cargo clippy -p ffs-core --all-targets --no-deps -- -D warnings : PASS\n  - cargo test -p ffs-core write_create_and_read_roundtrip_via_vfs_root_alias -- --nocapture : PASS\n- Also unblocked remote builds by adjusting crates/ffs-repair/src/codec.rs decode error mapping to avoid hard-coding newer DecodeError variants unavailable on workers resolving asupersync v0.2.0.","created_at":"2026-02-18T04:19:38Z"},{"id":241,"issue_id":"bd-2s4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Closure evidence (CalmGorge, 2026-02-18): all parent-child beads under bd-2s4 are now closed, including bd-2s4.7 (RW persistence) and bd-2s4.5 (SIGKILL crash-consistency E2E). Latest deterministic run artifacts: artifacts/e2e/20260218_042005_ffs_ext4_rw_smoke/run.log (PASSED, duration 9s) and artifacts/e2e/20260218_042005_ffs_ext4_rw_smoke/sigkill_crash/recovery_verify_report.json (errors=[]; baseline_verified=500; fsynced_records=25). Gate checks for touched runtime surfaces executed via rch: cargo check/clippy/test for ffs-cli passed. Acceptance criteria satisfied: production rw mount path validated, clean unmount/remount persistence validated, SIGKILL recovery validated, errno path and lifecycle tests already closed in child beads.","created_at":"2026-02-18T09:22:37Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2s4.1","title":"Implement FUSE write operations dispatch (create/mkdir/write/unlink/rename)","description":"# Implement FUSE write operations dispatch

## GOAL
Add write operation handlers to FrankenFuse so that mounted filesystems support mutation operations.

## BACKGROUND
Currently ffs-fuse only handles read operations (getattr, lookup, readdir, read, readlink). Write operations need:
1. FUSE protocol handlers in FrankenFuse
2. Delegation to FsOps trait methods
3. Transaction wrapping (begin → operation → commit)
4. Error mapping for write-specific errors (ENOSPC, EROFS, EEXIST, etc.)

## DELIVERABLES
### Phase 1: File creation and writing
1. `create(parent, name, mode, umask, flags)` → create file + open
2. `write(ino, fh, offset, data, flags)` → write data to file
3. `setattr(ino, mode, uid, gid, size, atime, mtime)` → chmod/chown/truncate

### Phase 2: Directory operations  
4. `mkdir(parent, name, mode, umask)` → create directory
5. `unlink(parent, name)` → remove file
6. `rmdir(parent, name)` → remove directory
7. `rename(parent, name, newparent, newname, flags)` → move/rename

### Phase 3: Link operations
8. `link(ino, newparent, newname)` → hard link
9. `symlink(parent, name, link)` → symbolic link
10. `mknod(parent, name, mode, rdev)` → device/fifo/socket

### Each handler must:
- Check read-only mode → EROFS
- Begin MVCC transaction
- Delegate to FsOps implementation
- Commit or abort transaction
- Map errors to errno
- Log operation at appropriate level

## TESTS (REQUIRED)
1. Unit: create + write + read roundtrip
2. Unit: mkdir + create file inside + readdir
3. Unit: unlink reduces link count
4. Unit: rmdir on non-empty → ENOTEMPTY
5. Unit: rename across directories
6. Unit: EROFS on read-only mount
7. Unit: ENOSPC when disk full (simulated)
8. Integration: 100 concurrent file creates
9. Property: Random create/delete sequences maintain consistency

## LOGGING REQUIREMENTS
### Operation-level logging (all at debug unless error)
- create (debug): parent_ino, name, mode, result_ino
- write (trace): ino, offset, len, written_bytes (not data content)
- setattr (debug): ino, changed_fields (mode/size/time), old_values, new_values
- mkdir (debug): parent_ino, name, mode, result_ino
- unlink (debug): parent_ino, name, target_ino, remaining_links
- rmdir (debug): parent_ino, name, target_ino
- rename (debug): old_parent, old_name, new_parent, new_name, flags

### Transaction logging
- txn_begin (trace): txn_id, operation, inode(s)
- txn_commit (trace): txn_id, commit_seq, duration_us
- txn_abort (debug): txn_id, reason

### Error logging
- write_error (warn): operation, ino, errno, error_context
- permission_denied (info): operation, ino, uid, required_mode
- disk_full (warn): operation, requested_blocks, available_blocks

### Performance logging
- slow_operation (info): operation, ino, duration_ms (if > 100ms)

## POSIX COMPLIANCE NOTES
- create: EEXIST if exists and O_EXCL, ENOSPC if disk full
- mkdir: EEXIST, EACCES for permission
- unlink: EISDIR (use rmdir instead), ENOENT
- rmdir: ENOTEMPTY, ENOTDIR
- rename: atomic replace semantics (POSIX.1-2017)

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. All 10 FUSE write operations implemented
2. Transaction wrapping correct (commit on success, abort on error)
3. Read-only mount correctly rejects writes with EROFS
4. Errno mapping matches POSIX expectations
5. All operations logged with sufficient context for debugging","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:52:34.624724548Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T19:18:08.152211645Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T19:18:08.152137927Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2s4.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2ad","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:53:01.857993659Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2s4.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2s4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:52:34.624724548Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2s4.1","depends_on_id":"bd-huh.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:53:01.951296010Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":44,"issue_id":"bd-2s4.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"POSIX COMPLIANCE: Each write operation must handle specific error cases: create → EEXIST if exists, ENOSPC if disk full; mkdir → EEXIST, EACCES; unlink → EISDIR (use rmdir instead), ENOENT; rmdir → ENOTEMPTY, ENOTDIR; rename → atomic replace semantics. Reference POSIX.1-2017 for exact behavior.","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:57:58Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2s4.2","title":"Implement FUSE setxattr and removexattr operations","description":"# Implement FUSE setxattr and removexattr operations

## GOAL
Enable writing and removing extended attributes through the FUSE mount interface.

## BACKGROUND
listxattr and getxattr are already implemented (read-only). This bead adds the write side:
- setxattr: Create or replace an extended attribute
- removexattr: Delete an extended attribute

Requires inline xattr space management (may need to promote to external block if inline space exhausted).

## DELIVERABLES
1. FrankenFuse::setxattr() handler
2. FrankenFuse::removexattr() handler
3. Inline xattr space management (check if fits, promote if needed)
4. Integration with MVCC transaction wrapping

## TESTS
1. Set xattr on file, read it back
2. Replace existing xattr with larger value
3. Remove xattr, verify gone
4. Overflow inline space → external block (or ENOSPC if not supported)
5. EROFS on read-only mount

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. POSIX xattr semantics preserved
2. Transaction-wrapped for crash safety
3. No data corruption on inline/external boundary","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GentleElk","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:54:39.295902137Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-17T20:11:54.424340962Z","closed_at":"2026-02-17T20:11:54.424320825Z","close_reason":"Implemented FUSE setxattr/removexattr with core ext4 xattr mutation wiring, ENOSPC fallback, RO semantics, and tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2s4.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2s4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:54:39.295902137Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2s4.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2s4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:54:39.398307753Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":183,"issue_id":"bd-2s4.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).

Mapped graveyard sections: 0.5 Graceful Degradation + 10.3 Learning-Augmented Online + 12.13 Change-point
EV score: 16 (Impact*Confidence*Reuse / (Effort*Friction))
Priority tier: A
Budgeted mode: queue_budget=1024, throttle_sleep_ms<=25, shed_ratio_cap=0.15
Fallback trigger: deterministic threshold gate with Emergency=read-only

Required proof artifacts for execution on this bead:
- Baseline + hotspot evidence before changes.
- Isomorphism proof block per lever.
- Evidence-ledger event for decision path + fallback invocation.
- Repro pack: env.json, manifest.json, repro.lock (and LEGAL.md if IP risk appears).","created_at":"2026-02-13T23:09:29Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-tbn","title":"Implement btrfs transaction model with MVCC integration","description":"# Implement btrfs transaction model with MVCC integration

## GOAL
Implement btrfs transaction boundaries integrated with FrankenFS MVCC, bridging native btrfs semantics with our version-chain model.

## BACKGROUND
btrfs transactions are fundamentally different from ext4/JBD2:
- Transactions are tree-level, not journal-level
- Commit creates new tree roots
- Old roots preserved for snapshots
- This naturally maps to MVCC!

The key insight: btrfs transaction = MVCC transaction boundary.

## TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS

### 1. Transaction Handle
```rust
pub struct BtrfsTransaction {
    /// MVCC transaction ID
    txn_id: TxnId,
    /// Snapshot for reads
    snapshot: Snapshot,
    /// Pending tree modifications
    pending_trees: BTreeMap<TreeId, TreeRoot>,
    /// Delayed refs accumulated
    delayed_refs: DelayedRefQueue,
    /// New blocks allocated
    allocated: Vec<BlockNumber>,
    /// Blocks to free on commit
    to_free: Vec<BlockNumber>,
}
```

### 2. Begin/Commit/Abort
```rust
impl BtrfsTransaction {
    pub fn begin(store: &MvccStore, cx: &Cx) -> Result<Self>;
    
    pub fn commit(self, cx: &Cx) -> Result<CommitSeq> {
        // 1. Flush delayed refs
        // 2. Update super block with new roots
        // 3. MVCC commit (FCW check)
        // 4. Persist to device
    }
    
    pub fn abort(self) {
        // Free allocated blocks
        // Discard pending changes
    }
}
```

### 3. Tree Root Updates
On commit:
- New tree roots written
- Super block updated with generation and roots
- Checksum tree updated
- Free space cache updated

### 4. Compatibility Mode
For mounting existing btrfs images:
- Read existing transaction log
- Initialize MVCC from latest committed generation
- Subsequent writes use MVCC

## TESTS
1. Unit: Begin/abort discards changes
2. Unit: Begin/commit persists changes
3. Integration: Concurrent transactions with disjoint trees
4. Integration: FCW conflict detection for same tree
5. Recovery: Crash before commit -> rollback

## LOGGING
- Transaction begin (debug): txn_id, generation
- Transaction commit (info): duration, trees modified
- Transaction abort (warn): reason

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. ACID semantics preserved
2. Concurrent writers do not corrupt
3. Crash recovery to last committed state
4. MVCC snapshots see consistent trees","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:51:55.750691013Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T21:33:04.103301376Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T21:33:04.103251292Z","close_reason":"Implemented btrfs transaction model over MVCC in ffs-btrfs with begin/commit/abort semantics, delayed-ref flush, tree-root staging, FCW conflict coverage, and workspace gates passing. Remaining core on-disk wiring is tracked as broader write-path follow-up.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["btrfs","core","mvcc"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-tbn","depends_on_id":"bd-1b7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:54:13.509360853Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-tbn","depends_on_id":"bd-29z","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:56:21.021338413Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-tbn","depends_on_id":"bd-3sj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:54:13.626558426Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":30,"issue_id":"bd-tbn","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"FEATURE_PARITY mapping: This covers 'btrfs transaction parity' (currently ❌).
